text: Ever wondered how new species are born? Let’s dive into allopatric speciation. Allopatric speciation happens when populations are separated by physical barriers, like mountains or rivers. But here's the twist... over time, those isolated groups adapt to their unique environments. These adaptations can lead to genetic changes, and eventually, they become distinct species! So, speciation is nature’s incredible way of creating diversity, but it’s not always clear-cut. Curious for more? Hit that follow button and explore the wonders of biology with us!
